stories.title: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana moved to Broadmoor
stories.summary:
Rudakubana is serving a 52-year sentence after the fatal stabbing of three girls and the attempted murder of eight other children and two adults at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in Southport, Merseyside, in July 2024.

The killer, who was 17 at the time of the attack, pleaded guilty to killing Alice da Silva Aguiar, nine, Bebe King, six, and Elsie Dot Stancombe, seven.

Rudakabana was transferred from the category A prison to Broadmoor high-security hospital on Thursday in a move that does not change his sentence, the MoJ said.

stories.title: Sabrina Ionescu teases 3-point contest rematch with Warriors star Steph Curry
stories.summary: If there is one thing Warriors star Steph Curry and four-time WNBA All-Star selection Sabrina Ionescu have in common, it would be their elite shooting prowess from 3-point range. The two sharpshooters previously faced off in a 3-point competition during the 2024 NBA All-Star Weekend , where Curry slightly edged out Ionescu with a 29-26 final score. The New York Liberty star won the 2025 WNBA 3-Point Contest the following year, but told the New York Post’s Madeline Kenney that she is uncertain about participating in the competition during this season’s WNBA All-Star Weekend. However, Ionescu teased she would consider a rematch with Curry for another NBA-WNBA cross-league 3-point contest.

stories.title: Irish premier Micheál Martin to meet Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelensky in Kyiv
stories.summary: The two leaders are expected to discuss Russia’s war against Ukraine and how Ireland’s presidency of the Council of the EU can support Ukraine’s sovereignty. Mr Martin will also meet Ukraine’s new prime minister Serhii Koretskyi, former head of state energy company Naftogaz, who was appointed last week during a controversial government reshuffle. Mr Zelensky has cited Mr Koretskyi’s record in the energy sector and said he was best prepared to guide Ukraine through another winter, when Russian attacks on the power grid intensify.

stories.title: Oprah Winfrey sparks fresh backlash after she revisits Prince Harry and Meghan Markle bombshell interview
stories.summary: Oprah Winfrey is facing a new wave of backlash over an old royal bombshell. After revisiting her 2021 interview with Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, Winfrey's explanation of her "silent or silenced" question has sparked renewed debate online. The media mogul's reflection on one of the most talked-about moments from the explosive sit-down has reignited debate over the interview that shook the monarchy, divided viewers and put Harry and Meghan's claims about royal life under a global microscope.

stories.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
stories.summary: In a dramatic twist on Love Island, host Maya Jama confronted the Islanders about their relationships, leading to accusations of 'gameplay' among the contestants. Three couples—Aidan and Priya, Lorenzo and Julia, and Samraj and Mica—are now at risk of elimination after being voted the most vulnerable during a podcast segment. Viewers will find out which couple faces the boot in Friday's episode.

stories.title: Fuzhou's jasmine tea industrial heritage fuels local cultural tourism growth
stories.summary: Workers make jasmine tea at a company of a jasmine tea industrial park in Fuzhou, southeast China's Fujian Province, on July 22, 2026. Fuzhou's traditional jasmine tea scenting craftsmanship, a national intangible cultural heritage, is driving the city's cultural tourism development. Leveraging its century-old jasmine tea industrial heritage, the city has restored historic tea plantations and scenting workshops while developing tea-themed industrial, educational and cultural attractions. In recent years, the renowned jasmine tea heritage has been transformed into a vibrant driver of the city's cultural tourism growth.

stories.title: The Glen Iris restaurant that balances substance with style
stories.summary: 161 Burke Rd Glen Iris Press PLAY to listen to Fuller’s full review of Bar Sophia Have you ever walked into a place early, just to kill time, and accidentally discovered somewhere you knew you’d be coming back to properly? That is exactly what Bar Sophia in Glen Iris feels like. It is the kind of restaurant that suits a few different people at once: locals who want something elevated but still relaxed, wine lovers who enjoy discovering something a little off-centre, and anyone who values service that feels both polished and genuinely human.

stories.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
stories.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

stories.title: How to see the Perseids meteor shower this summer
stories.summary: Up to 100 meteors per hour can typically be seen during the Perseids shower. July 21, 2026, 4:43 PM It's the time of the summer when the Perseids meteor shower lights up the night sky. The Perseids occur every year from about July 17 to Aug. 23 and typically peak around mid-August, according to the American Meteor Society . This year, the Perseids are expected to peak between Aug. 12 and 13, according to AMS. The Perseids meteor shower is known for its frequency of meteors – capable of producing between 50 and 100 visible meteors per hour in a clear dark sky, according to NASA .
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

stories.title: Pre-order EA SPORTS FC™ 27
stories.summary: Digital render of Kylian Mbappé along with a logo of EA SPORTS FC™ 27. is now the current item in the media gallery The World’s Game. Watch the full EA SPORTS FC™ 27 reveal on July 23. Subscribe to see more from EA SPORTS FC: http://bit. ly/pzw4VD Available on Sep 24, 2026 - 11 PM UTC On EA app starting from You are buying a license to access this digital content per the EA User Agreement . and Terms Of Sale apply. This game includes optional in-game purchases of virtual currency that can be used to acquire virtual in-game items including a random selection of virtual in-game items.

stories.title: Mohamed Salah is one step away from joining Besiktas.. New developments in the deal
stories.summary: Ankara, Turkey – Turkish club Besiktas is close to finalizing a deal to sign Egyptian star Mohamed Salah during the current summer transfer window, after negotiations witnessed new developments in recent hours. The negotiations had stalled due to a dispute over the commission of Ramy Abbas, Mohamed Salah’s agent, as Turkish reports indicated that he had requested a percentage of 35% of the total value of the contract, while Besiktas insisted that the commission should not exceed 15%.

stories.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
stories.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

stories.title: Pete Davidson Is Unrecognizable in Mobster Transformation for Tommy Karate
stories.summary: Pete Davidson Raves About Ex Kim Kardashian as "Superhuman" Live from New York, it’s… Pete Davidson ? The Saturday Night Live alum appeared totally transformed in a first look at his portrayal of mobster Tommy Pitera in the upcoming film Tommy Karate . In a picture released July 23, Davidson traded in his signature smile for a dark smolder as he looked off camera to show his profile view. He also seemingly wore a wig with the hair brushed back and a dark suit in the black and white photo. The movie’s director Justin Chon shared the pic and quipped about Davidson’s departure from his comedic past. “Petey’z all growns up now,” he wrote on Instagram .

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump warns of unprecedented ‘massive attack’ on Iran
stories.summary: Live updates, Kuwait says fire caused by Iranian attack on Al-Abdali border contained as Bahrain comes under fire again. Video Duration 11 minutes 54 seconds 11:54 'No better way' to end US-Iran war than diplomacy: Martin Griffiths Published On 23 Jul 2026 Jordan, Bahrain and Kuwait have reported retaliatory missile and drone attacks from Iran after a 12th consecutive day of US strikes, including an attack on Kuwait’s Al-Abdali border crossing with Iraq. US President Trump says he is considering a “massive attack” on Iran, “bigger than ever before”, and that he is close to making a decision.

stories.title: Representative Angie King Tours Grand Lake Health System’s Outpatient Renovation Project
stories.summary: ST. MARYS, Ohio – Grand Lake Health System was honored to welcome Ohio State Representative Angie King for a tour of its ongoing Outpatient Renovation Project, highlighting the organization’s continued investment in expanding access to high-quality healthcare for the communities it serves. Representative Angie King/District 84; Cindy Berning, CEO; Tyler Kroeger, VP of Operations; Holly Jacomet, VP of Quality and Physician Practices; Gabe Wolf, Legislative Aide, District 84; Lana Hinders, CNO; Tricia Pohl, CFO During her visit, Representative King toured the newly completed Phase II of the renovation project and received an overview of the remaining phases.

stories.title: Ajax Turner Senior Center board splits, takes conflicting votes, starts over
stories.summary: After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans. CLARKSVILLE, TN ( CLARKSVILLE NOW ) – After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, with resignations and restarts, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans, stating it will move forward with taking over the Ajax Turner Senior Center operations. But several questions are still unanswered. The City of Clarksville said Monday the Senior Citizens Center at 953 Clark St.

stories.title: Director Simon Farrell awarded 30,500 options at Serina (SER)
stories.summary: Director Simon Farrell awarded 30,500 options at Serina (SER) (Neutral) (Neutral) Form Type 4 Serina Therapeutics, Inc. reported that director Simon Farrell received a grant of stock options covering 30,500 shares of common stock. The options have an exercise price of $2. 1700 per share , expire on 2036-07-22 , and vest in substantially equal yearly installments over three years beginning on the first anniversary of the grant date, subject to his continued service. Following this grant, Farrell holds 30,500 stock options directly. Positive None. Negative None. Insider Simon Farrell Role Director Type Security Shares Price Value Grant/Award Stock Option (right to buy) F1 30,500 $0. 00 $0.

stories.title: Trump scoffs at ‘affordability’ as he touts economic policies at Georgia rally
stories.summary: Donald Trump scoffed at the word “affordability” as a political message, describing it at a campaign rally on Wednesday as a term concocted by Democratic political consultants to criticize his presidency. Yet even as the president dismissed the notion of affordability, he devoted much of his speech at Wheeler high school to touting the bread-and-butter benefits of his economic policies. “So I just want you to remember that if you’re paying a little bit more – a lot of it’s come down now, we got it coming down very rapidly actually,” Trump said in Marietta, an Atlanta suburb, casting higher prices today as a lingering effect of the Biden administration.

stories.summary: Showing stats for Brasileiro Serie A 2026 Brasileiro Serie A 2026 Brasileiro Serie A 2026 l w l w w Form w l w d w 18 Matches Played 18 6 Won 4 6 Drawn 6 6 Lost 8 13 League Position 19 Home Draw Away Match Odds Corinthians Results Brasileiro Serie A 2026 30. 05. 2026 1 - 3 Gre (a) 24. 05. 2026 1 - 0 Atl (h) 17. 05. 2026 3 - 1 Bot (a) 10. 05. 2026 3 - 2 Sao (h) 04. 05. 2026 2 - 1 Mir (a) 26. 04. 2026 1 - 0 Vas (h) 19. 04. 2026 0 - 0 Vit (a) 12. 04. 2026 0 - 0 Pal (h) 05. 04. 2026 0 - 1 SC (h) 02. 04. 2026 3 - 1 Flu (a) 22. 03. 2026 1 - 1 Fla (h) 20. 03. 2026 0 - 0 Cha (a) 15. 03. 2026 1 - 1 San (a) 12. 03. 2026 0 - 2 Cor (h) 25. 02. 2026 1 - 1 Cru (a) 19. 02. 2026 0 - 1 Par (a) 12. 02.

stories.title: 5-Part Dragon Series Was The Best Fantasy On TV Before Game Of Thrones
stories.summary: Published Jul 21, 2026, 2:00 PM EDT Cathal Gunning has been writing about movies, television, culture, and politics online and in print since 2017. He worked as a Senior Editor in Adbusters Media Foundation from 2018-2019 and wrote for WhatCulture in early 2020. He has been a Senior Features Writer for ScreenRant since 2020. Although one underrated adventure series from 2008 was once one of the best fantasy shows in TV history, the arrival of Game of Thrones knocked this BBC show from its throne fairly fast. Some TV shows are simply destined to change the medium’s history forever with their arrival.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: China’s Moonshot AI allegedly stole advanced AI capabilities from US firm Anthropic to develop its K3 model, a White House adviser claimed on Wednesday. The adviser said Moonshot accessed restricted Nvidia servers and used a technique called distillation to extract answers from stronger AI models. Moonshot’s K3, unveiled last week, has been praised for narrowing the gap between Chinese and Western AI technology.

stories.title: Derry's European fate in the balance after narrow defeat in Croatia
stories.summary: Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 22:01 Derry face a 1-0 deficit in their home return leg Derry City's Europa Conference League tie against HNK Rijeka is still in the balance after the first leg, at a rain-soaked Stadion HNK. Croatian international Toni Fruk's second half goal was enough to separate the sides, but City boss Tiernan Lynch will take heart that his side weren't troubled for long periods and will hoping they can have another memorable night at the Ryan McBride Brandywell Stadium, for next Thursday night's second leg. The Candystripes produced a super professional first half and nullified the Croatia men's threat, while in the final 10 minutes they slowly grew into the contest.

stories.title: How Arantxa Sanchez Vicario’s $41 million tennis fortune ‘vanished’ as her life fell apart
stories.summary: Arantxa Sanchez Vicario was once the World No. 1 tennis player. Now, Sanchez Vicario is penniless after her ex-husband drained her $41 million fortune, she claims . “I focused on playing tennis and never handled my own financial affairs because I didn’t know how. I trusted my husband… And the [£31m] vanished. I was left to shoulder the entire burden,” Sanchez Vicario recently said on Spanish TV show “Universo Callejo,” according to The Sun . Sanchez Vicario and her ex-husband, Josep Santacana, were found guilty of fraud in January 2024. The couple allegedly hid assets in an attempt to avoid paying large debts to the Banque de Luxembourg.

stories.title: Javier Bardem Reveals the Perks of Working With Wife Penelope Cruz: ‘There’s a Level of Trust’
stories.summary: Javier Bardem and Penélope Cruz will star as a married couple in the upcoming film Bunker, set for U.S. release in early 2027. The actors, who have been married since 2010, praised the trust and chemistry they bring to the project, directed by Florian Zeller. Bardem described the film as an intimate, humorous, and powerful story about marriage and ambition.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
stories.summary: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The US is imposing new tariffs on around 60 trading partners accounting for the vast majority of its imports over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The duties, ranging from 10% to 12. 5%, target key economic partners – including the UK, China, EU, Canada, Japan and India. They come in on Friday, as a temporary 10% tax on foreign goods introduced earlier this year expires. The move is the latest escalation in the global trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year.

stories.title: Charli xcx Cranks Up the Guitars and Launches Her Post-‘Brat’ Era With the Razor-Sharp ‘Music, Fashion, Film’: Album Review
stories.summary: When an artist is as prolific — and as easily bored — as Charli xcx , “How much is too much? ” becomes a significant question, and one she herself addressed in “The Moment,” the mock-umentary on her “Brat” era released earlier this year. That remarkably self-aware film, in which she plays a sort of parallel-universe version of herself, finds her comically grappling with overexposure and the insatiable yet fickle demands of 21st century entertainment, with its domineering creative directors, executives and influencers.

stories.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
stories.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

stories.title: President Macron talks Modesto in meeting with George Lucas. What happened in Paris
stories.summary: Get the Eat. Sip. Play. newsletter in your inbox Available now: Sign up here for the best food, drink and entertainment coverage in Stanislaus County. Modesto native George Lucas received a prestigious award from French President Emmanuel Macron this month. Lucas was one of five in the movie industry honored during a ceremony July 15 in Paris, according to a story on Variety. com . Along with Lucas, actresses Jodie Foster and Sigourney Weaver and Illumination founder and CEO Chris Meledandri were presented with insignias of Knight of the Legion of Honor, the story said, and French filmmaker Claude Lelouch received the insignia of Commander of Legion of Merit.

stories.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
stories.summary: A federal health advisory panel has recommended easing restrictions on several controversial peptides, voting 8-6 to allow pharmacies to produce injectables like BPC-157 and TB-500. This decision, which is not binding, could lead to increased marketing of these substances by telehealth companies, despite concerns from government scientists about their safety and efficacy. The move aligns with Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s support for peptides, potentially reversing previous FDA regulations.

stories.title: Shaking Up the Emmys By Moving Five Categories Out of the Telecast Might Not Work, But at Least the TV Academy Is Trying Something
stories.summary: Richard Gadd, change your flight plans. The creator and star of HBO Max’s “Half Man” wound up with the series’ only Emmy nomination this year — via his supporting actor in a limited/anthology series or movie nod. But as of last week, that category will no longer be presented during NBC’s Emmy telecast on Sept. 14. Last Thursday, the TV Academy Board of Governors voted to move five categories to the earlier Emmy ceremonies (no longer dubbed the “Creative Arts Emmys ,” now they’re just “Emmys”): writing for a variety series, supporting actor and actress in a limited series or movie, directing for a limited series or movie and writing for a limited series or movie.

stories.title: ‘Drag Race’ stars threaten to ‘sue’ Nancy Mace over attack on drag queens
stories.summary: Republican Rep. Nancy Mace launched a new attack on drag queens this week, but the Drag Race divas are hitting back. The South Carolina conservative drew attention during a congressional hearing when she displayed photographs of Drag Race host RuPaul and stars Kandy Muse and Joey Jay as she argued that drag “sexualizes” children. In a subcommittee hearing Tuesday, examining the Trump administration’s assertion that the Smithsonian is insufficiently patriotic, Republican Rep. Tim Burchett said the institution had "become infected by a woke ideology. " Mace criticized the National Museum of American History for displaying a photo of drag legend and Emmy-winner RuPaul.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battle back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby. Betfred Super League Hull FC (12) 20 Tries: Batchelor, D Litten, Arthur Goals: Hardaker 4 Hull KR (12) 28 Tries: Amone, Mourgue, Broadbent, Burgess, Dezaria Goals: Mourgue 7 Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battled back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby.

stories.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
stories.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

stories.title: Iran Rejects U.S. Cease-Fire Deal Presented by Iraqi Leader
stories.summary: The details of the proposal were not immediately clear, but Iranian officials said that it was the only offer on the table.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: Trump’s New Demand for Nuclear Deal Puts Saudis in a Bind
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia has long said that a condition of normalizing relations with Israel would be a pathway toward Palestinian statehood, a prospect that Israel’s current government has rejected.

stories.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
stories.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence". Wangchuk, a well-known name in India, ended his hunger strike after the government assured him no legal action would be taken against protesters rallying at Janta Mantar site, India's NDTV website reported. India's state and health ministers had visited Wangchuk in hospital. Reacting to Wangchuk's decision to end his hunger strike, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke wrote in a post on X: "We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days.

stories.title: Murder of Thomas in Crépol: the court upholds the aggravating circumstance of racism – Interview
stories.summary: The investigating judge in Valence has reclassified the charges against the suspects in the murder of Thomas Perotto in Crépol. The magistrates have added an aggravating circumstance to the charges against the accused. This decision comes after lengthy debates that have characterized the investigation since its inception. A debated legal classification The aggravating circumstance cited relates to the fact that the victims were allegedly attacked because they were white and French citizens. This legal classification had been the subject of much debate since the beginning of the investigation. Thomas Perotto was killed in 2023 in an event that deeply shocked the public.

stories.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
stories.summary: App is installed through Windows Update when certain LG monitors connect to a PC. Broader concerns Users have been finding LG Monitor App Installer and its ads on their systems without LG ever showing a prompt or asking for permission. LG has some of the most expensive computer monitors available. Paying, in some cases, over $1,000 for a monitor that ends up forcing ads onto Windows is disruptive and a privacy concern. Once the app is installed, “LG technically possesses permission to use ‘all system resources,’” as well as to “collect geolocation, device data, online activity, contacts, user credentials, transactions, and more,” Burke said.

stories.title: U.S. Withdraws Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
stories.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times called an attempt to intimidate the free press.

stories.title: Should Travelers Give Up on Summer in Western Europe Because of the Heat?
stories.summary: A favorite vacation option may be at risk because of rising temperatures. Even those sticking with their summer plans agree: More air conditioning would help.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
stories.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear. Shares of Google and Tesla plunged on Thursday as investors were spooked by the ever-increasing amounts of money being spent on artificial intelligence (AI). Google's parent company Alphabet saw its share price drop by nearly 7%, while Elon Musk's electric vehicle-maker Tesla saw its stock fall 14. 5%. Both reported negative free cash flow - the money retained after paying for operations and investments - in financial results on Wednesday, alongside promises to spend billions more in the months and years to come.

stories.title: India Emerges as Fifth-Largest Military Spender Amid Global Surge
stories.summary: India has climbed to become the world’s fifth-largest military spender, with its defence expenditure reaching $92. 1 billion in 2025. The increase marks an 8. 9 per cent rise from the previous year and reflects a broader global trend of rising military budgets. The latest data comes from the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute , which released its annual report on global military spending. The report highlights a continued escalation in defence investments across nations, driven by geopolitical tensions and regional conflicts.

stories.title: Magnicity unveils reimagined euromast to boost rotterdams appeal for international travellers
stories.summary: Experiential 2 min Read Magnicity has reopened the renovated Euromast in Rotterdam following an extensive refurbishment. The transformed attraction introduces immersive storytelling, upgraded dining and enhanced observation experiences while targeting growing international markets, including India. The company aims to position the landmark as a key addition to premium leisure and MICE itineraries across the Netherlands. Magnicity has unveiled the newly renovated Euromast in Rotterdam, introducing a refreshed visitor experience that combines immersive attractions, upgraded hospitality and panoramic views of the Dutch city.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

stories.title: Tyler Deutscher named senior director of operations at NCK Medical Center
stories.summary: Posted Thursday, July 23, 2026 12:58 pm CONCORDIA, Kan. — North Central Kansas Medical Center (NCKMC) has named Tyler Deutscher as its new senior director of operations, effective August 3, 2026. Deutscher will succeed LeRoy Dickinson, who retired from the position in April. Deutscher has served as director of rehabilitation services at NCKMC for the past seven years. In his new role, he will help oversee hospital operations and continue working to support the delivery of quality health care services to patients throughout North Central Kansas.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: BBC Inside Science
stories.summary: Will the US army benefit from testing its male soldiers for testosterone levels? Home News Sport Weather iPlayer Sounds Bitesize CBeebies CBBC Food Home News Sport Business Technology Health Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Video Live Use BBC. com or the new BBC App to listen to BBC podcasts, Radio 4 and the World Service outside the UK. Find out how to listen to other BBC stations Episode details Radio 4 , · 23 Jul 2026 , · 28 mins Available for 35 days Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth has announced that the US military will be screening military personnel aged 30 and older for testosterone deficiency.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: I was once visiting Massachusetts in the US and forgot to take my card into the gas station before filling up ( ‘A guy did a runner in a Jaguar’: the petrol theft scandal – and the garage owner struggling to stop it, 21 July ). After some loudspeaker remonstration, I took my card in, apologising that I’d forgotten as we don’t do that in the UK. The owner almost fell on the floor laughing. “How do you stop people stealing gas? ” he asked. “We don’t,” I replied. Further merriment ensued. Sadly, it’s time we did start not releasing pumps until we have cash or a card deposit on hand.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

offbeat.title: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana moved to Broadmoor
offbeat.summary:
Rudakubana is serving a 52-year sentence after the fatal stabbing of three girls and the attempted murder of eight other children and two adults at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in Southport, Merseyside, in July 2024.

The killer, who was 17 at the time of the attack, pleaded guilty to killing Alice da Silva Aguiar, nine, Bebe King, six, and Elsie Dot Stancombe, seven.

Rudakabana was transferred from the category A prison to Broadmoor high-security hospital on Thursday in a move that does not change his sentence, the MoJ said.

offbeat.title: Sabrina Ionescu teases 3-point contest rematch with Warriors star Steph Curry
offbeat.summary: If there is one thing Warriors star Steph Curry and four-time WNBA All-Star selection Sabrina Ionescu have in common, it would be their elite shooting prowess from 3-point range. The two sharpshooters previously faced off in a 3-point competition during the 2024 NBA All-Star Weekend , where Curry slightly edged out Ionescu with a 29-26 final score. The New York Liberty star won the 2025 WNBA 3-Point Contest the following year, but told the New York Post’s Madeline Kenney that she is uncertain about participating in the competition during this season’s WNBA All-Star Weekend. However, Ionescu teased she would consider a rematch with Curry for another NBA-WNBA cross-league 3-point contest.

offbeat.title: Irish premier Micheál Martin to meet Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelensky in Kyiv
offbeat.summary: The two leaders are expected to discuss Russia’s war against Ukraine and how Ireland’s presidency of the Council of the EU can support Ukraine’s sovereignty. Mr Martin will also meet Ukraine’s new prime minister Serhii Koretskyi, former head of state energy company Naftogaz, who was appointed last week during a controversial government reshuffle. Mr Zelensky has cited Mr Koretskyi’s record in the energy sector and said he was best prepared to guide Ukraine through another winter, when Russian attacks on the power grid intensify.

offbeat.title: Oprah Winfrey sparks fresh backlash after she revisits Prince Harry and Meghan Markle bombshell interview
offbeat.summary: Oprah Winfrey is facing a new wave of backlash over an old royal bombshell. After revisiting her 2021 interview with Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, Winfrey's explanation of her "silent or silenced" question has sparked renewed debate online. The media mogul's reflection on one of the most talked-about moments from the explosive sit-down has reignited debate over the interview that shook the monarchy, divided viewers and put Harry and Meghan's claims about royal life under a global microscope.

offbeat.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
offbeat.summary: In a dramatic twist on Love Island, host Maya Jama confronted the Islanders about their relationships, leading to accusations of 'gameplay' among the contestants. Three couples—Aidan and Priya, Lorenzo and Julia, and Samraj and Mica—are now at risk of elimination after being voted the most vulnerable during a podcast segment. Viewers will find out which couple faces the boot in Friday's episode.

offbeat.title: Fuzhou's jasmine tea industrial heritage fuels local cultural tourism growth
offbeat.summary: Workers make jasmine tea at a company of a jasmine tea industrial park in Fuzhou, southeast China's Fujian Province, on July 22, 2026. Fuzhou's traditional jasmine tea scenting craftsmanship, a national intangible cultural heritage, is driving the city's cultural tourism development. Leveraging its century-old jasmine tea industrial heritage, the city has restored historic tea plantations and scenting workshops while developing tea-themed industrial, educational and cultural attractions. In recent years, the renowned jasmine tea heritage has been transformed into a vibrant driver of the city's cultural tourism growth.

offbeat.title: The Glen Iris restaurant that balances substance with style
offbeat.summary: 161 Burke Rd Glen Iris Press PLAY to listen to Fuller’s full review of Bar Sophia Have you ever walked into a place early, just to kill time, and accidentally discovered somewhere you knew you’d be coming back to properly? That is exactly what Bar Sophia in Glen Iris feels like. It is the kind of restaurant that suits a few different people at once: locals who want something elevated but still relaxed, wine lovers who enjoy discovering something a little off-centre, and anyone who values service that feels both polished and genuinely human.

offbeat.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
offbeat.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

offbeat.title: How to see the Perseids meteor shower this summer
offbeat.summary: Up to 100 meteors per hour can typically be seen during the Perseids shower. July 21, 2026, 4:43 PM It's the time of the summer when the Perseids meteor shower lights up the night sky. The Perseids occur every year from about July 17 to Aug. 23 and typically peak around mid-August, according to the American Meteor Society . This year, the Perseids are expected to peak between Aug. 12 and 13, according to AMS. The Perseids meteor shower is known for its frequency of meteors – capable of producing between 50 and 100 visible meteors per hour in a clear dark sky, according to NASA .
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

offbeat.title: Pre-order EA SPORTS FC™ 27
offbeat.summary: Digital render of Kylian Mbappé along with a logo of EA SPORTS FC™ 27. is now the current item in the media gallery The World’s Game. Watch the full EA SPORTS FC™ 27 reveal on July 23. Subscribe to see more from EA SPORTS FC: http://bit. ly/pzw4VD Available on Sep 24, 2026 - 11 PM UTC On EA app starting from You are buying a license to access this digital content per the EA User Agreement . and Terms Of Sale apply. This game includes optional in-game purchases of virtual currency that can be used to acquire virtual in-game items including a random selection of virtual in-game items.

offbeat.title: Mohamed Salah is one step away from joining Besiktas.. New developments in the deal
offbeat.summary: Ankara, Turkey – Turkish club Besiktas is close to finalizing a deal to sign Egyptian star Mohamed Salah during the current summer transfer window, after negotiations witnessed new developments in recent hours. The negotiations had stalled due to a dispute over the commission of Ramy Abbas, Mohamed Salah’s agent, as Turkish reports indicated that he had requested a percentage of 35% of the total value of the contract, while Besiktas insisted that the commission should not exceed 15%.

offbeat.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
offbeat.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

offbeat.title: Pete Davidson Is Unrecognizable in Mobster Transformation for Tommy Karate
offbeat.summary: Pete Davidson Raves About Ex Kim Kardashian as "Superhuman" Live from New York, it’s… Pete Davidson ? The Saturday Night Live alum appeared totally transformed in a first look at his portrayal of mobster Tommy Pitera in the upcoming film Tommy Karate . In a picture released July 23, Davidson traded in his signature smile for a dark smolder as he looked off camera to show his profile view. He also seemingly wore a wig with the hair brushed back and a dark suit in the black and white photo. The movie’s director Justin Chon shared the pic and quipped about Davidson’s departure from his comedic past. “Petey’z all growns up now,” he wrote on Instagram .

offbeat.title: Iran war live: Trump warns of unprecedented ‘massive attack’ on Iran
offbeat.summary: Live updates, Kuwait says fire caused by Iranian attack on Al-Abdali border contained as Bahrain comes under fire again. Video Duration 11 minutes 54 seconds 11:54 'No better way' to end US-Iran war than diplomacy: Martin Griffiths Published On 23 Jul 2026 Jordan, Bahrain and Kuwait have reported retaliatory missile and drone attacks from Iran after a 12th consecutive day of US strikes, including an attack on Kuwait’s Al-Abdali border crossing with Iraq. US President Trump says he is considering a “massive attack” on Iran, “bigger than ever before”, and that he is close to making a decision.

offbeat.title: Representative Angie King Tours Grand Lake Health System’s Outpatient Renovation Project
offbeat.summary: ST. MARYS, Ohio – Grand Lake Health System was honored to welcome Ohio State Representative Angie King for a tour of its ongoing Outpatient Renovation Project, highlighting the organization’s continued investment in expanding access to high-quality healthcare for the communities it serves. Representative Angie King/District 84; Cindy Berning, CEO; Tyler Kroeger, VP of Operations; Holly Jacomet, VP of Quality and Physician Practices; Gabe Wolf, Legislative Aide, District 84; Lana Hinders, CNO; Tricia Pohl, CFO During her visit, Representative King toured the newly completed Phase II of the renovation project and received an overview of the remaining phases.

offbeat.title: Ajax Turner Senior Center board splits, takes conflicting votes, starts over
offbeat.summary: After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans. CLARKSVILLE, TN ( CLARKSVILLE NOW ) – After a set of meetings in which two versions of the Senior Citizens Center Board took conflicting actions, with resignations and restarts, the City of Clarksville provided an update on its plans, stating it will move forward with taking over the Ajax Turner Senior Center operations. But several questions are still unanswered. The City of Clarksville said Monday the Senior Citizens Center at 953 Clark St.

offbeat.title: Director Simon Farrell awarded 30,500 options at Serina (SER)
offbeat.summary: Director Simon Farrell awarded 30,500 options at Serina (SER) (Neutral) (Neutral) Form Type 4 Serina Therapeutics, Inc. reported that director Simon Farrell received a grant of stock options covering 30,500 shares of common stock. The options have an exercise price of $2. 1700 per share , expire on 2036-07-22 , and vest in substantially equal yearly installments over three years beginning on the first anniversary of the grant date, subject to his continued service. Following this grant, Farrell holds 30,500 stock options directly. Positive None. Negative None. Insider Simon Farrell Role Director Type Security Shares Price Value Grant/Award Stock Option (right to buy) F1 30,500 $0. 00 $0.

offbeat.title: Trump scoffs at ‘affordability’ as he touts economic policies at Georgia rally
offbeat.summary: Donald Trump scoffed at the word “affordability” as a political message, describing it at a campaign rally on Wednesday as a term concocted by Democratic political consultants to criticize his presidency. Yet even as the president dismissed the notion of affordability, he devoted much of his speech at Wheeler high school to touting the bread-and-butter benefits of his economic policies. “So I just want you to remember that if you’re paying a little bit more – a lot of it’s come down now, we got it coming down very rapidly actually,” Trump said in Marietta, an Atlanta suburb, casting higher prices today as a lingering effect of the Biden administration.

offbeat.title: Corinthians v Clube do Remo Match Stats, Hit Rates & Bet Builder Data
offbeat.summary: Showing stats for Brasileiro Serie A 2026 Brasileiro Serie A 2026 Brasileiro Serie A 2026 l w l w w Form w l w d w 18 Matches Played 18 6 Won 4 6 Drawn 6 6 Lost 8 13 League Position 19 Home Draw Away Match Odds Corinthians Results Brasileiro Serie A 2026 30. 05. 2026 1 - 3 Gre (a) 24. 05. 2026 1 - 0 Atl (h) 17. 05. 2026 3 - 1 Bot (a) 10. 05. 2026 3 - 2 Sao (h) 04. 05. 2026 2 - 1 Mir (a) 26. 04. 2026 1 - 0 Vas (h) 19. 04. 2026 0 - 0 Vit (a) 12. 04. 2026 0 - 0 Pal (h) 05. 04. 2026 0 - 1 SC (h) 02. 04. 2026 3 - 1 Flu (a) 22. 03. 2026 1 - 1 Fla (h) 20. 03. 2026 0 - 0 Cha (a) 15. 03. 2026 1 - 1 San (a) 12. 03. 2026 0 - 2 Cor (h) 25. 02. 2026 1 - 1 Cru (a) 19. 02. 2026 0 - 1 Par (a) 12. 02.

offbeat.title: 5-Part Dragon Series Was The Best Fantasy On TV Before Game Of Thrones
offbeat.summary: Published Jul 21, 2026, 2:00 PM EDT Cathal Gunning has been writing about movies, television, culture, and politics online and in print since 2017. He worked as a Senior Editor in Adbusters Media Foundation from 2018-2019 and wrote for WhatCulture in early 2020. He has been a Senior Features Writer for ScreenRant since 2020. Although one underrated adventure series from 2008 was once one of the best fantasy shows in TV history, the arrival of Game of Thrones knocked this BBC show from its throne fairly fast. Some TV shows are simply destined to change the medium’s history forever with their arrival.

offbeat.title: Derry's European fate in the balance after narrow defeat in Croatia
offbeat.summary: Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 22:01 Derry face a 1-0 deficit in their home return leg Derry City's Europa Conference League tie against HNK Rijeka is still in the balance after the first leg, at a rain-soaked Stadion HNK. Croatian international Toni Fruk's second half goal was enough to separate the sides, but City boss Tiernan Lynch will take heart that his side weren't troubled for long periods and will hoping they can have another memorable night at the Ryan McBride Brandywell Stadium, for next Thursday night's second leg. The Candystripes produced a super professional first half and nullified the Croatia men's threat, while in the final 10 minutes they slowly grew into the contest.

offbeat.title: How Arantxa Sanchez Vicario’s $41 million tennis fortune ‘vanished’ as her life fell apart
offbeat.summary: Arantxa Sanchez Vicario was once the World No. 1 tennis player. Now, Sanchez Vicario is penniless after her ex-husband drained her $41 million fortune, she claims . “I focused on playing tennis and never handled my own financial affairs because I didn’t know how. I trusted my husband… And the [£31m] vanished. I was left to shoulder the entire burden,” Sanchez Vicario recently said on Spanish TV show “Universo Callejo,” according to The Sun . Sanchez Vicario and her ex-husband, Josep Santacana, were found guilty of fraud in January 2024. The couple allegedly hid assets in an attempt to avoid paying large debts to the Banque de Luxembourg.

offbeat.title: Javier Bardem Reveals the Perks of Working With Wife Penelope Cruz: ‘There’s a Level of Trust’
offbeat.summary: Javier Bardem and Penélope Cruz will star as a married couple in the upcoming film Bunker, set for U.S. release in early 2027. The actors, who have been married since 2010, praised the trust and chemistry they bring to the project, directed by Florian Zeller. Bardem described the film as an intimate, humorous, and powerful story about marriage and ambition.

offbeat.title: President Macron talks Modesto in meeting with George Lucas. What happened in Paris
offbeat.summary: Get the Eat. Sip. Play. newsletter in your inbox Available now: Sign up here for the best food, drink and entertainment coverage in Stanislaus County. Modesto native George Lucas received a prestigious award from French President Emmanuel Macron this month. Lucas was one of five in the movie industry honored during a ceremony July 15 in Paris, according to a story on Variety. com . Along with Lucas, actresses Jodie Foster and Sigourney Weaver and Illumination founder and CEO Chris Meledandri were presented with insignias of Knight of the Legion of Honor, the story said, and French filmmaker Claude Lelouch received the insignia of Commander of Legion of Merit.

offbeat.title: ‘Drag Race’ stars threaten to ‘sue’ Nancy Mace over attack on drag queens
offbeat.summary: Republican Rep. Nancy Mace launched a new attack on drag queens this week, but the Drag Race divas are hitting back. The South Carolina conservative drew attention during a congressional hearing when she displayed photographs of Drag Race host RuPaul and stars Kandy Muse and Joey Jay as she argued that drag “sexualizes” children. In a subcommittee hearing Tuesday, examining the Trump administration’s assertion that the Smithsonian is insufficiently patriotic, Republican Rep. Tim Burchett said the institution had "become infected by a woke ideology. " Mace criticized the National Museum of American History for displaying a photo of drag legend and Emmy-winner RuPaul.

offbeat.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
offbeat.summary: Sonam Wangchuk, a supporter of youth-led protesters demanding education reforms, says he wants to avert "possible violence". Wangchuk, a well-known name in India, ended his hunger strike after the government assured him no legal action would be taken against protesters rallying at Janta Mantar site, India's NDTV website reported. India's state and health ministers had visited Wangchuk in hospital. Reacting to Wangchuk's decision to end his hunger strike, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke wrote in a post on X: "We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days.

offbeat.title: Murder of Thomas in Crépol: the court upholds the aggravating circumstance of racism – Interview
offbeat.summary: The investigating judge in Valence has reclassified the charges against the suspects in the murder of Thomas Perotto in Crépol. The magistrates have added an aggravating circumstance to the charges against the accused. This decision comes after lengthy debates that have characterized the investigation since its inception. A debated legal classification The aggravating circumstance cited relates to the fact that the victims were allegedly attacked because they were white and French citizens. This legal classification had been the subject of much debate since the beginning of the investigation. Thomas Perotto was killed in 2023 in an event that deeply shocked the public.

offbeat.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
offbeat.summary: App is installed through Windows Update when certain LG monitors connect to a PC. Broader concerns Users have been finding LG Monitor App Installer and its ads on their systems without LG ever showing a prompt or asking for permission. LG has some of the most expensive computer monitors available. Paying, in some cases, over $1,000 for a monitor that ends up forcing ads onto Windows is disruptive and a privacy concern. Once the app is installed, “LG technically possesses permission to use ‘all system resources,’” as well as to “collect geolocation, device data, online activity, contacts, user credentials, transactions, and more,” Burke said.

offbeat.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
offbeat.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear. Shares of Google and Tesla plunged on Thursday as investors were spooked by the ever-increasing amounts of money being spent on artificial intelligence (AI). Google's parent company Alphabet saw its share price drop by nearly 7%, while Elon Musk's electric vehicle-maker Tesla saw its stock fall 14. 5%. Both reported negative free cash flow - the money retained after paying for operations and investments - in financial results on Wednesday, alongside promises to spend billions more in the months and years to come.

offbeat.title: India Emerges as Fifth-Largest Military Spender Amid Global Surge
offbeat.summary: India has climbed to become the world’s fifth-largest military spender, with its defence expenditure reaching $92. 1 billion in 2025. The increase marks an 8. 9 per cent rise from the previous year and reflects a broader global trend of rising military budgets. The latest data comes from the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute , which released its annual report on global military spending. The report highlights a continued escalation in defence investments across nations, driven by geopolitical tensions and regional conflicts.

offbeat.title: Magnicity unveils reimagined euromast to boost rotterdams appeal for international travellers
offbeat.summary: Experiential 2 min Read Magnicity has reopened the renovated Euromast in Rotterdam following an extensive refurbishment. The transformed attraction introduces immersive storytelling, upgraded dining and enhanced observation experiences while targeting growing international markets, including India. The company aims to position the landmark as a key addition to premium leisure and MICE itineraries across the Netherlands. Magnicity has unveiled the newly renovated Euromast in Rotterdam, introducing a refreshed visitor experience that combines immersive attractions, upgraded hospitality and panoramic views of the Dutch city.

offbeat.title: Tyler Deutscher named senior director of operations at NCK Medical Center
offbeat.summary: Posted Thursday, July 23, 2026 12:58 pm CONCORDIA, Kan. — North Central Kansas Medical Center (NCKMC) has named Tyler Deutscher as its new senior director of operations, effective August 3, 2026. Deutscher will succeed LeRoy Dickinson, who retired from the position in April. Deutscher has served as director of rehabilitation services at NCKMC for the past seven years. In his new role, he will help oversee hospital operations and continue working to support the delivery of quality health care services to patients throughout North Central Kansas.

crisis.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
crisis.summary: China’s Moonshot AI allegedly stole advanced AI capabilities from US firm Anthropic to develop its K3 model, a White House adviser claimed on Wednesday. The adviser said Moonshot accessed restricted Nvidia servers and used a technique called distillation to extract answers from stronger AI models. Moonshot’s K3, unveiled last week, has been praised for narrowing the gap between Chinese and Western AI technology.

crisis.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
crisis.summary: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The US is imposing new tariffs on around 60 trading partners accounting for the vast majority of its imports over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The duties, ranging from 10% to 12. 5%, target key economic partners – including the UK, China, EU, Canada, Japan and India. They come in on Friday, as a temporary 10% tax on foreign goods introduced earlier this year expires. The move is the latest escalation in the global trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year.

tech.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
tech.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

tech.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
tech.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

entertainment.title: Charli xcx Cranks Up the Guitars and Launches Her Post-‘Brat’ Era With the Razor-Sharp ‘Music, Fashion, Film’: Album Review
entertainment.summary: When an artist is as prolific — and as easily bored — as Charli xcx , “How much is too much? ” becomes a significant question, and one she herself addressed in “The Moment,” the mock-umentary on her “Brat” era released earlier this year. That remarkably self-aware film, in which she plays a sort of parallel-universe version of herself, finds her comically grappling with overexposure and the insatiable yet fickle demands of 21st century entertainment, with its domineering creative directors, executives and influencers.

entertainment.title: Shaking Up the Emmys By Moving Five Categories Out of the Telecast Might Not Work, But at Least the TV Academy Is Trying Something
entertainment.summary: Richard Gadd, change your flight plans. The creator and star of HBO Max’s “Half Man” wound up with the series’ only Emmy nomination this year — via his supporting actor in a limited/anthology series or movie nod. But as of last week, that category will no longer be presented during NBC’s Emmy telecast on Sept. 14. Last Thursday, the TV Academy Board of Governors voted to move five categories to the earlier Emmy ceremonies (no longer dubbed the “Creative Arts Emmys ,” now they’re just “Emmys”): writing for a variety series, supporting actor and actress in a limited series or movie, directing for a limited series or movie and writing for a limited series or movie.

sports.title: Littler cruises into Matchplay semis with win over Rock
sports.summary: World number one Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continues with a 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool, as he averages 109. 57. Luke Littler's World Matchplay title defence continued with a straightforward 16-7 quarter-final win over Josh Rock in Blackpool. Rock initially kept pace with the world number one as the players traded breaks to reach 5-5. But after 19-year-old Littler reeled off five legs in a row, there proved to be no way back for Rock as the defending champion, in dazzling form, cruised into the semi-finals. After throwing 17 180s with a scoring average of 109. 57, Littler said: "At the minute, everything's going to plan.

sports.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
sports.summary: Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battle back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby. Betfred Super League Hull FC (12) 20 Tries: Batchelor, D Litten, Arthur Goals: Hardaker 4 Hull KR (12) 28 Tries: Amone, Mourgue, Broadbent, Burgess, Dezaria Goals: Mourgue 7 Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battled back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby.

sports.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
sports.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

health.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
health.summary: A federal health advisory panel has recommended easing restrictions on several controversial peptides, voting 8-6 to allow pharmacies to produce injectables like BPC-157 and TB-500. This decision, which is not binding, could lead to increased marketing of these substances by telehealth companies, despite concerns from government scientists about their safety and efficacy. The move aligns with Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s support for peptides, potentially reversing previous FDA regulations.

health.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
health.summary: I was once visiting Massachusetts in the US and forgot to take my card into the gas station before filling up ( ‘A guy did a runner in a Jaguar’: the petrol theft scandal – and the garage owner struggling to stop it, 21 July ). After some loudspeaker remonstration, I took my card in, apologising that I’d forgotten as we don’t do that in the UK. The owner almost fell on the floor laughing. “How do you stop people stealing gas? ” he asked. “We don’t,” I replied. Further merriment ensued. Sadly, it’s time we did start not releasing pumps until we have cash or a card deposit on hand.

health.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
health.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

breaking.title: Iran Rejects U.S. Cease-Fire Deal Presented by Iraqi Leader
breaking.summary: The details of the proposal were not immediately clear, but Iranian officials said that it was the only offer on the table.

breaking.title: Trump’s New Demand for Nuclear Deal Puts Saudis in a Bind
breaking.summary: Saudi Arabia has long said that a condition of normalizing relations with Israel would be a pathway toward Palestinian statehood, a prospect that Israel’s current government has rejected.

facts.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
facts.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.

facts.title: Should Travelers Give Up on Summer in Western Europe Because of the Heat?
facts.summary: A favorite vacation option may be at risk because of rising temperatures. Even those sticking with their summer plans agree: More air conditioning would help.

facts.title: BBC Inside Science
facts.summary: Will the US army benefit from testing its male soldiers for testosterone levels? Home News Sport Weather iPlayer Sounds Bitesize CBeebies CBBC Food Home News Sport Business Technology Health Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Video Live Use BBC. com or the new BBC App to listen to BBC podcasts, Radio 4 and the World Service outside the UK. Find out how to listen to other BBC stations Episode details Radio 4 , · 23 Jul 2026 , · 28 mins Available for 35 days Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth has announced that the US military will be screening military personnel aged 30 and older for testosterone deficiency.

politics.title: U.S. Withdraws Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
politics.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times called an attempt to intimidate the free press.

politics.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
politics.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

politics.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
politics.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.
